{{Quote|I ''led'' the Imperial forces at the Battle of Callos.|Juno Eclipse, to Starkiller|<ref name="TFU">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' graphic novel]]</ref>}}
The '''Bombing of Callos''', also known as the '''Battle of Callos''', occurred early during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]].

==The battle==
{{Quote|With respect, sir, it would be genocide to maintain the bombardment—a completely unnecessary waste of life. They are already beaten.|Juno Eclipse, to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="TFUN">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref>}}
In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] under the command of the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]],<ref name="kenner">{{KennerCite|set=Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (toy line)|pack=Darth Vader vs. Prince Xizor|link=soteKxizor-vader.asp}}</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], attacked Callos, after discovering evidence that the [[Callosian]]s were developing a new offensive technology intended to oppose those who were spreading the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. [[Black Eight Squadron]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] personal fighter wing, was assigned to bomb the Callosian defenses.

Once the military objective had been neutralized, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]] was ordered to continue the assault. Eclipse protested, saying that maintaining the bombardment would amount to genocide, since Callos was already defenseless. Vader offered her an alternative, stating that if she disabled Callos's planetary reactor, he would consider the mission complete. Eclipse was initially relieved, believing that a precision strike upon a single target would cause much less collateral damage than blanket bombing the entire planet, and carried out her orders.<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed novelization">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]]</ref>

Although the bombing run on the reactor was a success, it came at a high cost: immediately following the attack, the damaged reactor began spewing pollutants into the atmosphere and flooding the planet's river systems with poisonous chemicals, triggering a chain reaction that would ultimately destroy Callos's biosphere.

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Callos was like this [Felucia]: overgrown, teeming with life. And we razed the planet. It's a dead world now.|Juno Eclipse remembering her role in the event|<ref name="TFU" />}}
Eclipse provided data on the attack to a friend of hers with a background in environmental science; he told her that the planet's forests would be gone within six months, and would never return.

Having a natural interest in xenobiology, Eclipse admired and was fascinated by the wide diversity of life to be found in the galaxy; the thought that she was personally responsible for the destruction of a planet that had previously been teeming with life made her feel physically ill. For months following the mission on Callos, she was haunted by feelings of guilt and remorse and sometimes experienced nightmares about leading the attack itself. 

Although she had demonstrated her loyalty, Lord Vader judged that Captain Eclipse lacked the ruthlessness that he deemed vital to the command of Black Eight Squadron, and reassigned her to pilot the ''[[Rogue Shadow]]''. She later remarked that the planet [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]], with its abundance of life, reminded her of [[Callos]].
